_meta.keyKnowledge: We are learning to deliver structured spoken texts for specific purposes and audiences, using formal language and effective vocal features.

_source.w: We are learning to deliver structured spoken texts for specific purposes and audiences, using formal language and effective vocal features.
y: Being able to speak formally and persuasively is a powerful skill in academic, professional and civic life.
Structuring our spoken texts helps our audience follow our argument or information clearly.
Practising formal presentations builds the confidence we need for high-stakes speaking situations.
eg: We plan and deliver a formal presentation with a clear thesis, supporting points and conclusion.
We use appropriate formal language, avoiding colloquialisms in academic presentations.
We employ vocal features — pace, pause, emphasis, volume — strategically to enhance our message.
We adapt our delivery for different audiences and contexts.
We use cue cards or notes effectively without reading directly from them.
We evaluate our own and peers' presentations against specific criteria.
